tasks.description: Create `packages/schemas/src/api/billing/downgrade-preview.schema.ts` with the `DowngradePreviewSchema` (accommodations: { cap, activeCount, excessCount, items[] }, promotions: same shape, photos: [], grandfatherFlags: [], hasExcess: boolean) and `KeepSelectionsSchema` ({ accommodationIds?, promotionIds?, photoKeepMap? }). Export both from the billing schemas barrel. Tests: `packages/schemas/src/api/billing/__tests__/downgrade-preview.schema.test.ts` covering valid full payload, valid minimal (hasExcess=false, empty arrays), invalid (missing cap, negative count). Files: 1 schema file + 1 test file.

tasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/protected/billing/subscriptions/downgrade-preview endpoint
tasks.description: Create `apps/api/src/routes/billing/protected/downgrade-preview.ts`. Handler reads `targetPlan` query param (required, validated via Zod), resolves `actor.id` from the session, calls `computeDowngradeExcess({ userId: actor.id, targetPlanSlug }, deps)` from `apps/api/src/services/subscription-downgrade-excess.service.ts:284`, maps result to `DowngradePreviewSchema`, returns 200. Auth: protected tier, no entitlement gate (read-only informational). Error handling: missing targetPlan → 400; unknown plan → 404; service error → 500. Register route in the protected billing router. Add row to `docs/billing/endpoint-gate-matrix.md`: Decision=none, Reason=read-only informational endpoint. Tests: `apps/api/src/routes/billing/protected/__tests__/downgrade-preview.test.ts` — valid response shape, 400 on missing param, 401 when unauthenticated. Files: 1 route file + 1 test file + endpoint-gate-matrix.md update.

subtasks.title: Implement route handler calling computeDowngradeExcess
Validate targetPlan query param with Zod
Register route in protected billing router
Add endpoint-gate-matrix.md row (Decision=none)
Write tests: valid shape, 400 missing param, 401 unauthenticated

tasks.title: Fix and extend billingApi in endpoints-protected.ts (web API client)
tasks.description: In `apps/web/src/lib/api/endpoints-protected.ts`, make four changes: (1) Add `billingApi.previewDowngrade({ targetPlan: string })` calling `GET /api/v1/protected/billing/subscriptions/downgrade-preview?targetPlan=<slug>`, returning `DowngradePreview`. (2) Fix `billingApi.changePlan()`: add `keepSelections?: KeepSelections` to the request body, add `X-Idempotency-Key` header generation (crypto.randomUUID()), correct return type to the discriminated union `PlanChangeResponse` (active | scheduled | pending_payment, with `restrictionPreview?` on the scheduled variant). (3) Fix `billingApi.cancelSubscription()`: repoint from the wrong `DELETE /billing/subscriptions/:id` to `POST /api/v1/protected/billing/subscriptions/:id/cancel` with body `{ reason?: string }`, return type `UserCancelSubscriptionResponse` ({ subscriptionId, cancelAtPeriodEnd, canceledAt, accessUntil }). (4) Extend the `SubscriptionData` type with `scheduledPlanChange?: { newPlanId, effectiveAt, restrictionPreview? }` and optional `restrictionPreview?` field. Audit callers of `cancelSubscription()` before repointing — confirm no other caller depends on the hard-cancel behavior. Tests: `apps/web/src/lib/api/__tests__/endpoints-protected.billing.test.ts` covering each changed function signature and return type shape. Files: 1 existing client file + 1 test file.

subtasks.title: Add previewDowngrade() method

Fix cancelSubscription() — repoint to POST soft-cancel, update return type
Extend SubscriptionData with scheduledPlanChange + restrictionPreview fields
Audit all cancelSubscription callers for hard-cancel dependency
Write tests for all changed signatures and return type shapes

tasks.title: Add scheduled-change banner to SubscriptionDashboard
tasks.description: In `apps/web/src/components/billing/SubscriptionDashboard.client.tsx` (or a new sibling component), consume the extended `scheduledPlanChange` field from `SubscriptionData`. When `scheduledPlanChange` is present, render a `ScheduledChangeBanner` showing: the target plan name, `effectiveAt` date formatted in the active locale, and (if `restrictionPreview` is present) a collapsed summary of what will be restricted. Use CSS Modules (`ScheduledChangeBanner.module.css`) for styling, not Tailwind. i18n keys under `account.pages.subscription.scheduledChange.*` (es/en/pt — handled fully in T-010). For this task: add the JSX structure with placeholder i18n keys and the module.css skeleton. Tests: `apps/web/src/components/billing/__tests__/ScheduledChangeBanner.test.tsx` — renders when scheduledPlanChange is present, hidden when absent, shows effectiveAt. Files: 1-2 component files + 1 CSS module + 1 test file.

tasks.title: Implement PlanPicker component and plan-change entry point
tasks.description: Create `apps/web/src/components/billing/PlanPicker.client.tsx`. Fetch the public plans catalog (`GET /api/v1/public/billing/plans`) on mount. Filter to plans the user can switch to (exclude current plan). Render plan cards showing name, price, and billing interval. On selection, emit the chosen `targetPlan` slug upward. Include a loading skeleton and error state. Use CSS Modules (`PlanPicker.module.css`). Wire the existing 'Upgrade' link in `SubscriptionDashboard` to open a plan-change modal/section that mounts `PlanPicker` instead of navigating to the public pricing page. i18n keys under `account.pages.subscription.planChange.*` (shell keys — full es/en/pt copy in T-010). Tests: `apps/web/src/components/billing/__tests__/PlanPicker.test.tsx` — renders plan list, highlights selected plan, calls onSelect with correct slug. Files: 1 component + 1 CSS module + 1 test file.

tasks.title: Implement cancel flow — wire cancel button to SPEC-147 soft-cancel API
tasks.description: In `apps/web/src/components/billing/SubscriptionDashboard.client.tsx` (and/or a new `CancelSubscriptionModal.client.tsx`), replace the existing cancel button that opens an email-redirect modal with a flow that: (1) Shows a confirmation modal with a reason input (optional). (2) Calls `billingApi.cancelSubscription()` (now pointing to the correct SPEC-147 soft-cancel endpoint after T-003). (3) On success, renders `UserCancelSubscriptionResponse` — shows 'Your subscription will cancel on <accessUntil>' copy. (4) On 404 (HOSPEDA_USER_CANCEL_ENABLED=false), degrades gracefully by keeping the email-redirect fallback (`mailto:info@hospeda.com`). (5) On other errors, shows an error message. Use CSS Modules for the modal. i18n keys under `account.pages.subscription.cancel.*` (shell keys — full es/en/pt copy in T-010). Tests: `apps/web/src/components/billing/__tests__/CancelSubscriptionModal.test.tsx` — happy path shows accessUntil, 404 shows email fallback, loading state during call. Files: 1-2 component files + 1 CSS module + 1 test file.

tasks.title: Implement DowngradePreviewRenderer and KeepIdsSelector components
tasks.description: Create two components: (1) `DowngradePreviewRenderer.tsx` — renders the `DowngradePreview` payload when `hasExcess=true`: accommodations excess (cap, activeCount, excessCount, items list), promotions excess, photos excess, grandfatherFlags. When `hasExcess=false`, renders a 'No items will be restricted' confirmation message. (2) `KeepIdsSelector.tsx` — given the excess items from `DowngradePreview`, renders a multi-select UI for the user to choose which accommodations / promotions / photos to keep (building a `KeepSelections` object). Defaults: pre-select all items up to the cap (filling from `keepByDefault` items first if available). On change, emits the updated `KeepSelections` upward. Both use CSS Modules (`*.module.css`). i18n keys: `account.pages.subscription.downgradePreview.*` (shell — full copy in T-010). Tests: `apps/web/src/components/billing/__tests__/DowngradePreviewRenderer.test.tsx` — renders excess counts, renders no-excess message; `KeepIdsSelector.test.tsx` — pre-selects up to cap, emits KeepSelections on change. Files: 2 component files + 2 CSS modules + 2 test files.

tasks.title: Implement plan-change confirm step and result state (active/scheduled/pending_payment)
tasks.description: Create `PlanChangeConfirm.client.tsx`. This is the final step of the plan-change flow: (1) Receives the selected `targetPlan`, `keepSelections` (from T-007), and `previewDowngrade` result. (2) Renders a confirm screen summarizing the plan change (from/to plan names, billing interval, effective date if scheduled). (3) On confirm: calls `billingApi.changePlan({ newPlanId, billingInterval, keepSelections })` with a generated `X-Idempotency-Key`. (4) Handles the discriminated-union response: `active` → shows 'Plan changed successfully'; `scheduled` → shows a scheduled-at-period-end banner (reuses `ScheduledChangeBanner` from T-004); `pending_payment` → redirects to `checkoutUrl`. (5) Loading state during the POST. Use CSS Modules. i18n keys under `account.pages.subscription.planChange.confirm.*` (shell keys — full copy in T-010). Tests: `apps/web/src/components/billing/__tests__/PlanChangeConfirm.test.tsx` — active response shows success, scheduled shows banner, pending_payment redirects. Files: 1 component + 1 CSS module + 1 test file.

subtasks.title: Render confirm summary (from/to plan, interval, effectiveAt)

Handle active response (success message)
Handle scheduled response (reuse ScheduledChangeBanner)
Handle pending_payment response (redirect to checkoutUrl)
Write tests for all three response branches

tasks.title: Wire full plan-change flow into SubscriptionDashboard
tasks.description: Assemble the complete plan-change multi-step flow within `SubscriptionDashboard.client.tsx` using a state machine (or simple step enum): Step 1 = PlanPicker (T-005) → Step 2 = DowngradePreviewRenderer + KeepIdsSelector (T-007, only when hasExcess) → Step 3 = PlanChangeConfirm (T-008). The flow must: call `billingApi.previewDowngrade` after Step 1 if the selected plan is a downgrade (target plan tier < current plan tier); skip the preview step entirely for upgrades. Handle the loading state between steps. Add a 'Back' navigation between steps. On completion or cancel, return to the dashboard view. Tests: `apps/web/src/components/billing/__tests__/PlanChangeFlow.test.tsx` — upgrade skips preview step, downgrade shows preview step, back navigation works. Files: 1-2 component files + 1 test file.

subtasks.title: Implement step state machine (picker → preview? → confirm)

tasks.title: Add i18n keys (es/en/pt) for all new subscription UI copy
tasks.description: Add all new i18n keys under `account.pages.subscription.*` in `packages/i18n/src/locales/{es,en,pt}/account.json` (or the relevant split file — check existing structure first). Keys needed: `scheduledChange.*` (banner: scheduledAt, planName, willBeRestricted, expandRestrictions), `planChange.*` (modal title, step labels, confirm button, success/scheduled/pending messages, back button), `planChange.confirm.*` (summary lines), `downgradePreview.*` (excess counts, keepSelect labels, keepAll, noExcess), `cancel.*` (modal title, reasonLabel, confirmButton, success with accessUntil, emailFallback). All three locales must be complete — no placeholder values in es (native), no missing keys in en/pt. Tests: verify no missing keys by running the i18n type-check or snapshot test already in the test suite. Files: 3 locale JSON files.

tasks.title: Write integration tests and execute staging smoke
tasks.description: Integration tests and manual staging smoke for the full SPEC-203 surface. (1) Integration test `apps/web/src/lib/api/__tests__/billing-plan-change-flow.integration.test.ts`: mock the protected API responses and assert the full flow (previewDowngrade → changePlan → correct state transition for each union variant). (2) Integration test `apps/api/src/routes/billing/protected/__tests__/downgrade-preview.integration.test.ts`: real DB call with a seeded user + target plan, verify the endpoint returns the correct DowngradePreview shape. (3) Manual staging smoke per SPEC-143 checklist (sections: plan change, cancel): execute against staging once the PR is green. File a sign-off entry in `.qtm/specs/SPEC-203-web-plan-management-ui/docs/staging-smoke-signoff.md`. This task is NOT done until the staging smoke sign-off is filed. Files: 2 integration test files + 1 smoke sign-off doc.
